mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in the unemployment rate below 7.5 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with steady payroll growth indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies equity risk appetite and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any rolling 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index reading above 100 reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while S&P 500 forward returns remain negative in more than 60% of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms underlying economic momentum, which sustains earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth remains positive while S&P 500 forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarterly observations over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index reading that implies an annualized inflation rate above 2.5 percent triggers fears of premature monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: High inflation readings coincide with positive S&P 500 forward returns in more than 65% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in personal savings and nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding household balance sheets and disposable income, which fuels consumption-driven equity rallies and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate a positive information coefficient greater than 0.05 over any 24-month out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces real GDP growth, it signals supply-side efficiency gains and margin expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate exceeds GDPC1 growth rate
failure_condition: The relative growth spread produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered periods over a 5-year walk-forward validation.
